summary: Homepage is v1 authority. Hero, standard definition, What Changes, Why It Matters, navigation, and architecture are locked.

fields.label: Purpose
fields.value: Execution governance infrastructure — validation, proof, and accountability before execution commits.

fields.label: Defined for
fields.value: Government, enterprise, investors, and governed AI execution.

hero: EXECUTIA is execution governance infrastructure — the standard that requires validation, proof, and accountability before execution commits.
Most systems execute first. Governance comes after.
standard_definition: Execution first. Control and proof follow.
Governance first. Execution commits only after validation, control, proof, and commitment.

items.title: Government
items.text: Regulated decisions need proof before execution — not after audit.

items.title: Enterprise
items.text: Leaders need one execution truth, not controls rebuilt after failure.

items.title: Investors
items.text: Capital requires provable governance before operational commitment.

items.title: AI
items.text: Autonomous systems must not act without governed execution.
